LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT NELLY GABUEVA, JD, LLM'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS OFF CALENDAR. DEFENDANT DID NOT FILE AND SERVE THE MOTION AT LEAST 16 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE DATE OF THE HEARING, AS REQUIRED BY CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1005(B). THE COURT ALSO NOTES THAT DEFENDANT MISREPRESENTED TO THE COURT THAT THE PARTIES HAD AGREED TO CONTINUE THE HEARING ON THE MOTION UNTIL JANUARY 21 OR JANUARY 29, 2021, WHEN ACCORDING TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL NO SUCH AGREEMENT WAS EVER REACHED OR EVEN DISCUSSED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Designate Defendant Nelly
Allen Gabueva As Vexatious Litigant And Enter A Pre-Filing
Order (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 391);
Off Calendar Apr-20-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ANDREI ROMANENKO, Esq. of Law Office of Andrei Romanenko (Tel: 415-997-8144) for plaintiff;
Defendant NELLY A. GABUEVA (Tel: 415-534-5473) is pro per.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MAKPAL SERIKABAYEVA's Motion To Designate Defendant Nelly Allen Gabueva As Vexatious Litigant And Enter A Pre-Filing Order (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 391).

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's unopposed motion to designate defendant Nelly Allen Gabueva as a vexatious litigant and enter a prefiling order is granted.

On March 23, 2023, Defendant was given until April 6, 2023 to file an opposition to the vexatious litigant motion. Defendant filed no opposition.

Defendant is found to be a vexatious litigant under CCP 391(b)(2) for litigating the same issues already decided January 19, 2021, again on March 24, 2021, October 24, 2022, and December 19, 2022. Applying section 391, subdivision (b)(2) to any litigant, whether plaintiff or defendant, who repeatedly litigates prior determinations, is consistent with the statutory purpose. (Thompson v. Ioane (2017) 11 Cal.App.5th 1180, 1200.)

Defendant will be prohibited from filing any new litigation in the courts of this state in propria persona without first obtaining leave from the presiding judge of the court where the proposed litigation is to be filed. Code Civ. Proc 391.7 (a)

Order signed. Pre-Filing Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Mar-23-2023 Continued To Apr-20-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MAKPAL SERIKABAYEVA's Motion To Designate Defendant Nelly Allen Gabueva As Vexatious Litigant And Enter A Pre-Filing Order (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 391).

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is continued to April 20, 2023. Based on defendant's filing of March 2, 2023, defendant erroneously believes that this litigation is stayed pursuant to CCP 391.6. But this motion can still proceed. (See CCP 391.6; CCP 391.3(b); CCP 391.7(a).) Plaintiff filed this motion under CCP 391.7(a). The stay provision of CCP 391.6 applies where a defendant files a motion under CCP 391.1. Defendant has until April 6, 2023 to file and serve a substantive opposition. Plaintiff's reply shall be filed and served by April 13, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Jan-09-2023 Continued To Mar-14-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Default Judgment Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jan-05-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment prove up hearing.

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:51:05 a.m. and concluding at 10:09:12 a.m.

Plaintiff Makpal Serikbayeva appearing represented by Andrei Romanenko, Esq. Plaintiff appears via Zoom Video Conference, is placed under oath, and testifies.
Defendant Nelly Allen Gabueva not appearing.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents synopsis of case.
Plaintiff seeks Interest of 7% from Mar-30-2017 to Jan-05-2023.

The Court examines MAKPAL SERIKBAYEVA.
The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 1 - 2 Page Document: Fee Agreement from International Law Group dated Mar-30-2017 with Receipt for Cash Payment in the Sum of $4,500.00

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 2 - Invoices Related to Costs Associated with this Case

The matter is submitted.

THE COURT FINDS:

1. Default was entered against Defendant Nelly Allen Gabueva on Apr-22-2020.
2. Plaintiff has proven all relevant elements of its complaint.
3. Defendant is not licensed to practice law and received funds to render services that it was not licensed to do.

Plaintiff's request for penalties in the amount of $2,500.00 pursuant to Business and Professions Code 17206 is DENIED. This code does not apply in this case.

By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Makpal Serikbayeva, and against Defendant Nelly Allen Gabueva, as follows:

1. Damages: $4,950.00
2. Prejudgment Interest: $2,000.00
3. Costs: $349.90

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $7,299.90

Proposed judgment to be signed after filing of Plaintiff's amended request for court judgment.

Off Calendar May-27-2021 Continued To Sep-15-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Verification And Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Apr-22-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Andrei Romanenko, Esq. of the Law Office of Andrei Romanenko, 415.997.8144, for the Plaintiff.
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

DEFENDANT NELLY GABUEVA, JD, LLM's Motion To Vacate Default.

Matter called.

There being no other appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Nelly Allen Gabueva's motion to vacate the default entered on April 22, 2020 is denied. On January 19, 2021, the Court entered an order denying Defendant's prior motion to set aside the same default, which was brought on the very same grounds. The instant motion fails even to mention that prior order, in direct violation of Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(b). That statute provides, "A party who originally made an application for an order which was refused in whole or in part, . . . , may make a subsequent application for the same order upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law, in which case it shall be shown by affidavit what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances or law are claimed to be shown." The instant motion makes no showing of "new or different facts, circumstances, or law"; even if it could, it does not offer any satisfactory explanation for failing to raise them earlier. (New York Times Co. v. Superior Court (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 206, 212; Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674, 689-691.) Accordingly, it must be denied. Defendant is warned that if she refiles the same motion a third time, or files other frivolous motions, the Court will not hesitate to award substantial monetary sanctions.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

Defendant Nelly Gabueva, Jd, Llm'S Motion To Set Aside Default: (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion, Motion To Set Aside Default; Memo Of Points And Authorities, Declaration In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:

Self-represented defendant, Nelly Allen (aka Nelly Allen Gabueva) ( ) appears on her own behalf as defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

On calendar:
DEFENDANT NELLY GABUEVA, JD, LLM Notice Of Motion, Motion To Set Aside Default

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT NELLY GABUEVA, JD, LLM's Motion To Set Aside Default entered on April 22, 2020 is denied.

First, Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) does not provide Defendant with a basis for relief. A motion for relief under that statute "shall . . . be made within a reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months, after the judgment, dismissal, order, or proceeding was taken." Default was entered on April 22, 2020. Defendant did not file this motion to set aside until November 25, 2020, more than six months later. "The six-month time limitation is jurisdictional; the court has no power to grant relief under section 473 once the time has lapsed." (Austin v. Los Angeles Unified School Dist. (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 918, 918; see also Manson, Iver & York v. Black (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 36, 42 ["The six-month time limit for granting statutory relief is jurisdictional and the court may not consider a motion for relief made after that period has elapsed . . . The six-month period runs from entry of default."].) Even if Defendant's motion could be deemed to have been filed on September 8, 2020, when she first improperly attempted to file it on insufficient statutory notice, her unexplained delay of more than four months is unreasonable as a matter of law. "A delay is unreasonable as a matter of law . . . when it exceeds three months and there is no evidence to explain the delay." (Minick v. City of Petaluma (2016) 3 Cal.App.5th 15, 34.)

Second, Code of Civil Procedure section 473.5 does not provide Defendant with a basis for relief. "When service of a summons has not resulted in actual notice to a party in time to defend the action and a default or default judgment has been entered against him or her in the action, he or she may serve and file a notice of motion to set aside the default or default judgment and for leave to defend the action." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 473.5(a).) However, section 473.5 only applies if there has been lack of actual notice. "We hold that the reference in Code of Civil Procedure section 473.5 to 'actual notice' means genuine knowledge of the party litigant." (Rosenthal v. Garner (1983) 142 Cal.App.3d 891, 895.) Defendant admits that she knew about the action on February 7, 2020. (Gabueva Decl., Aug. 31, 2020, par. 8.) Default was not entered until April 22. Defendant had actual notice of the action in time to defend.

Finally, extrinsic fraud does not provide Defendant with a basis for relief. "Relief is denied . . .if a party has been given notice of an action and has not been prevented from participating therein. He has had an opportunity to present his case to the court and to protect himself from mistake or from any fraud attempted by his adversary." (Kulchar v. Kulchar (1969) 1 Cal.3d 467, 472.) As explained above, Defendant was aware of the action at least as early as February 7, and Defendant does not show that Plaintiff somehow prevented her from filing a responsive pleading. That a civil harassment restraining order was granted restraining Plaintiff's attorney from any contact with Defendant did not preclude Plaintiff from effecting service through a process server or prevent Defendant from filing an answer or other response to the complaint.

Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com.

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NELLY GABUEVA, JD, LLM's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is Off calendar. Defendant did not file and serve the motion at least 16 court days before the date of the hearing, as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b). The Court also notes that Defendant misrepresented to the Court that the parties had agreed to continue the hearing on the motion until January 21 or January 29, 2021, when according to plaintiff's counsel no such agreement was ever reached or even discussed.
